title: To The Lord Hopton, On His Fight In Cornwall.
content:
Go on, brave Hopton, to effectuate that
    Which we, and times to come, shall wonder at.
    Lift up thy sword; next, suffer it to fall,
    And by that one blow set an end to all.
meaning:
"To The Lord Hopton, On His Fight In Cornwall." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
